item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can understand why this would struggle in 2015, but reading it in 2020 I think it's perfectly fine and entertaining as it is. Not everything has to have a backstory. Not everything has to have depth. Silliness isn't a sin. I'm glad this lived to see a more enlightened time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wondering what would happen if someone had an artificial hand of put their hands in the wrong holes.ie left hand in right hole and right hand in left

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was halarious holy shit.

item.title: Congratulations, Doctor J.
item.content:encoded:
You broke it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, simple, and cute. The humor and "twist" are on-point.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is the pun in the number?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. I like things like these that's just weird. Not even harmful, just weird. For no apparent reason. No need for some convoluted background story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of SCP-092. Yes, it's a straightforward joke with nothing more to it… and there's nothing wrong with that. If anything, I would recommend expanding on that- link it tentatively to Are We Cool Yet?, Doctor Wondertainment, or some other GOI, like this is the work of someone trying to make an impressive/interesting anomalous item and just… not succeeding. It's one of the million mistakes that someone made on the way to becoming an expert, and it just happens to have survived long enough for the Foundation to find before breaking down.

In fact, if you needed to make this creepy, there's your angle: give some hint that the person who made it wasn't satisfied, and is still out there, making god knows what else.

Upvoted because SCPs don't have to make you shit your pants.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On secomd thought.. gonna give the Hammy Sinister Skull an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really I'm surprised more people don't know the origins of the word sinister. Admittedly it's mostly an English (and other Latin-derived language) speaking pun, but it's pretty common knowledge.

+1 because I support the foundation having more objects that are just plain weird instead of actively malicious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't get it until I knew about the latin name for left. It was much funnier the second time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't even get the pun at first, and it still put a smile on my face. I'm not sure exactly why I like this one… but I do. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which may come from the thing that you already know if you have studied any martial arts with sparring: left-handed fighters have trained to fight the right-handed, whereas the opposite isn't usually the case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Siding with the pudding here: sinister & dexter really are two of the basic latin words everyone should know; if they don't, well here's their chance to learn.

On a side note, sinister meaning evil didn't come about the way a lot of people think- the original secondary meaning was that if you were doing something sinister, you were working in a cunning and subtle way. Its only during the era of (hypothetical) chivalry that "using intelligent tactics/strategy" and "dirty cheater" became conflated, and being sinister was suddenly a bad thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's adorable and funny. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-5482, formerly right-handed, had become left-handed.

Testing with other left-handed D-class personnel, including D-582, revealed no deviance from the formula above.

Are you trying to say that D-5482 was used again in experiments? Cause I don't see D-582 mentioned/introduced in anywhere else. Also, for consistency, try to have the same number of digits per D-class label.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have never suggested this before, and I probably never will again, but this should probably be a -J. It's literally a joke (quite a good one, by my warped sense of humour), but with nothing else to it. As a -J I'd upvote, but I don't really think I can bring myself to upvote a mainlister that is nothing but a pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there's a joke re: the page number?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm hoping that this will be kept at a + rating by newer users who don't think that they're above enjoying this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the one hand, this SCP suffers from a fatal case of 'the Description doesn't actually describe what the thing does". It makes no sense as an in-universe document - especially as there's no attempt at justification whatsoever - and that is usually a dealbreaker for me.

(I'm sort of wondering if this could do with a rewrite. Like, make use of the whole "clinical tone" thing. Describe the SCP with a very dry, very clinical line. Something technically descriptive, but so dull it'll just slide off the reader's mind - especially once they get to the Test Log's hamminess)

That said, that said… the SCP amuses me, which is what it sets out to accomplish anyway? Call me cheap, but I like it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Based entirely off of a pun, and it's only competently executed enough to stay on the mainlist at a low rating. It could be better and actually funny while still maintaining a realm of seriousness, but it's just barely there, and just barely good enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did I miss that? Now, I see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And its opposite is dexter, yes.

(There's a reason I said deasil engine. I didn't misspell anything.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It took me a while and a bit of google to get it.
This would be better as a Joke skip, but still a tentative upvote on my part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
on the other hand….
I see what you did there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sinister comes from sinistra, which is Latin for left.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is the origin of right=good, left=bad symbolism ("Left Hand of Darkness", "Red Right Hand" "Right Hand Man"). Also found in heraldry ("Bar Sinister1", "Lion Dexter Argent") which definitely appear in A Song of Ice and Fire (the TV version of which I gather is somewhat popular), the words "ambidextrous", " dexterous", "dexterity" and "sinister", Mr. Sinister in Marvel comics and Jeff Lindsay's eponymous "good" serial killer Dexter Morgan. Also as I said the funk band Sinister Dexter, and the British comic book Sinister Dexter.

ETA- also the "dex" in pre-IUPAC names for things like dextrose and dextroamphetimine.

Footnotes
1. Which was a great Goth club in Hollywood, I don't think it is still there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only know the most basic of Latin. Like, I know a few scientific names and other sparse, random things. As I said before, I think this is true for most of the site's readership.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. I thought sinister/dexter was reasonably well known. References are pretty common from heraldry (and therefore mediaevalist fantasy) to the funk band Sinister Dexter to the classic 2000 AD comic book to Darkly Dreaming Dexter. I would think that between the popularity of A Game of Thrones and Dexter there would be some exposure.
  2. I am well aware of the etymology of "sinister" and this still wasn't funny. Just obvious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't made of transparent glass or plastic, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah! Should this stick around, it might actually behoove the author to adopt that terminology, because I was at least able to Google it! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like the gloves in a clean box, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is directed at both Myriad and sirpudding, but I don't feel like doing two replies:

Thank you. That makes sense and is kind funny, but it's really only for people who know at least cursory Latin. I can't imagine a majority of our readership knowing enough Latin, so I can't support this in good faith.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not terribly interesting, though I appreciate the hamminess. Moreso, I'm confused by the description of the gloves. I cannot picture this thing at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's too straightforward for its own good, and not much going on for it beyond the Hammy Sinister Skull.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On the one hand, I don't really like the execution. On the other hand, it's kinda amusing in a really stupid way…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 9 up, 9 down. This one's going to have an interesting life, I see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The box means "left-handed" when it says "sinister" but the trappings and tone imply "of evil intent".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sinister is Latin for 'left handed'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see the pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Guess it was just a Freudian slip, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OH MY GOD THAT WASN'T AN ORGASM

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The box getting an orgasm from the ambidextrous D-Class made me chuckle. I'll upvote this, but it honestly seems like it may have worked better as a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh… Oh my god, yes.

I don't know whether it's because I'm sleep deprived and running on dry fumes at 4am or whether I've just came from a chat in #site19 about Skeletor, but this article… Speaks to me on some level. Confusedly upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know what to say about it. It is both too short and too straightforward for me to like it, and I could predict what was going to happen since the very first test log.

On the other hand, it somehow managed to catch my attention long enough to finish it and I'd lie if I said it didn't make me smile a bit. However, that only saves a -J article and this is a mainlister.

No votr

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I find this SCP really amusing, I have to agree with it being a little empty in that it does one thing and is now apparently broken because of trying to process an ambidextrous hand? Did whoever built this really not consider that possibility?

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though its anomalous properties are presumed to be no longer extant, any personnel interacting with SCP-2741 should avoid inserting their hands into the device unless explicitly instructed otherwise.

Presumed to be? I dont think the concept of "We'll class it Safe, and we think its not anomalous anymore, but better not use it just in case…" Is a good idea. I'm certain the Foundation would be definitive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens if it's powered by a deasil engine instead of a AA battery?

SCP-2741: "ULTIMATE SINISTER, HYPER SINISTER, LUDICROUS SINISTER!"

D-05281's right hand was observed to morph into an extraneous left hand. Additional testing required.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seemed pretty clear what was going on pretty early and then the same thing just kept being reinforced and repeated, to no surprising effect or interesting tangent. -1.

Dr. J smacking the box in attempt to silence it seems silly, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely recommend reading and comparing the Moth Helmet. The basic idea of a box that does nothing more than make you left-handed isn't bad, in and of itself, and the item doesn't have to be particularly useful (it's actually a bit more interesting because it isn't). However, as it stands, the object begins and ends with just the pun, and that's not enough to carry the story.

Some possible ways to expand on it:

  • Why would a box that makes you left-handed exist?
  • Did someone get the definition of "sinister" wrong in its development?
  • Did someone get the definition of "sinister" right in its development?
  • Why is it so hammy?
  • What happens if it's powered by a deasil engine instead of a AA battery?

It doesn't need to be long (and probably best if it's short). But we need more than just the one pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't amusing, just predictable and repetitive, and since no other information is given about this object other than what it does, it is incredibly boring as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While it is certainly a good concept to play around with, its current execution falters. The characterisation of the SCP as a hammy box that does X just turns me off.

EDIT: In retrospect, I find this funny. So I will at least remove my downvote here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes this entire SCP is based off that one pun. Even the page number.

I love this with all my heart and I hope you will too.
